narrative.headline: Watch the transmission first on the 2020 Explorer
narrative.summary: Powertrain issues dominate owner complaints for this Explorer model year, with transmission cooling system failures reported while the vehicle is in motion being a recurring theme. Electrical faults and engine failures also appear frequently, with some owners describing sudden unintended acceleration events and at least one complete engine seizure on a near-new vehicle. Backup camera malfunctions round out the most-reported issues, with owners noting the display goes blank during reversing.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: Owners report transmission cooling system failures occurring while the vehicle is in motion, with some cases serious enough to prompt vehicle replacement. · 6 crash · 2 injured

issues.sample: TL* THE CONTACT OWNS A 2020 FORD EXPLORER. WHILE THE VEHICLE WAS PARKED, THE ENGINE SUDDENLY BEGAN TO REV VERY HIGHLY AND THE VEHICLE ATTEMPTED TO ACCELERATE FORWARD. THE DRIVER DEPRESSED THE BRAKE PEDAL TO PREVENT THE VEHICLE FROM MOVING. THE TRANSMISSION WAS SWITCHED INTO NEUTR
issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Owners report the engine suddenly revving to high RPM while parked, with the vehicle attempting to accelerate forward unexpectedly. · 4 fire · 3 crash · 2 injured

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Owners report complete engine seizure occurring on vehicles with very low mileage, leaving occupants stranded. · 4 fire · 2 crash

issues.title: Back Over Prevention
issues.blurb: Owners report the backup camera intermittently displaying a blank blue screen during reversing, providing no visual or warning feedback. · 8 crash · 1 injured

issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: 80 reports (7%), but the highest injury count here at 6, plus 4 crashes - some owners describe reduced stopping power. Test the brakes carefully and have a mechanic inspect them before buying.
